title: ferray-io: save writes element-by-element — no bulk write_all on contiguous data
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

NpyElement::write_slice for numeric types writes each element individually: 'for &val in data { writer.write_all(&val.to_ne_bytes())?; }'. For a contiguous f64 array of 1M elements, this does 1M separate write_all calls of 8 bytes each. NumPy writes the entire data buffer in a single fwrite call. Since the data is contiguous and in native byte order, we can write the entire slice as a single byte slice: unsafe { writer.write_all(std::slice::from_raw_parts(data.as_ptr() as *const u8, data.len() * size_of::<T>())) }. This would give orders-of-magnitude improvement for large arrays.
